title: StatefulSets, DaemonSets, Jobs, CronJobs, and Controller Selection · Cluster Case: K8s Statefulset Identity Storage
summary: A data intake system has interchangeable APIs, identity-bound workers, node agents, finite migrations, and scheduled reconciliation tasks requiring different controllers. Planned artifact: Add and verify the k8s statefulset identity storage increment in controller selection and completion contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Design StatefulSet ordinal identity, headless discovery, stable claims, ordering, update partition, scaling, and failure recovery around application guarantees.
Choose roll forward or rollback from causal evidence while coordinating schemas, config, traffic, durable state, compatibility, and post-recovery verification.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example platform runs a durable database as a Deployment, a node log agent as one replica, and a finite migration as a long-running Pod. Choose Deployment, StatefulSet, DaemonSet, Job, or CronJob from identity, placement, completion, concurrency, and retry contracts. Competency probe: Design StatefulSet ordinal identity, headless discovery, stable claims, ordering, update partition, scaling, and failure recovery around application guarantees. A defensible solution applies K8s Statefulset Identity Storage, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to controller selection and completion contract.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-worked-k8s-workload-controller-selection-5
  - competency: k8s-worked-k8s-workload-controller-selection-5
    artifact: controller-selection.yaml
    decision: worked k8s workload controller selection 5
    environment: managed canary cluster with provider-specific load balancing
    fault-injection: drop one metrics series during an autoscaling surge
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: worked5
    change: |
      stateless-api: Deployment
      identity-bound-worker: StatefulSet
      node-agent: DaemonSet
      one-time-migration: Job
      scheduled-reconcile: CronJob
      selection-proof: identity-completion-placement-update
    verify: kubectl get deploy,sts,ds,job,cronjob -n intake -o json
    failure: Reject Deployment-for-everything, exactly-once Job claims, CronJob clock certainty, or StatefulSet as automatic data replication.
    repair: Select from identity, completion, placement, update, storage, retry, idempotency, and failure evidence; then test controller-specific recovery.
content.caption: Runnable Kubernetes object and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: StatefulSet automatically replicates application data and makes every database highly available. This fails the K8s Statefulset Identity Storage contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
